combined_reviews:
Review 1
Rating: 1/5
Title: Big Disappointment- Gen 2 Echo8 is a Nightmare!
Text: This was purchased to replace an Echo 5 that I loved, but had been bricked after the latest over -the- air update from Amazon. It was ruined by the update and never recovered. This one is not anything like my original. There is a giant digital clock that I cannot change… the old echo five had a setting for this- the background adjustment control has also disappeared! Now it is called clock and photo display- and there is NO setting for background! I already had a photo for my background in my Alexa app and added it to the new Echo 8- but it does not show as a background choice now, just part of a rotating photo display, which in my case I do not want. I am unable to get the background to stay on instead it disappears and the only thing showing is the giant clock numbers which I do NOT want to see! I tried every setting but as I said the settings in new version removed any background and clock setting as was available in the old Alexa app and on screen settings. There are constant rotations of things even though I have turned off everything or told this new Echo to hide them- I just want the screen to show my background photo and the little clock in the top left corner like my old echo5 did. The new one insists on showing me recipes ( I Don’t cook) or a song that I played weeks and weeks ago and don’t want to see or hear about UNTIL I ask the Echo for it. Finally, I had to unplug this thing last night as the screen would not and I repeat, would not ever remove that darn clock! It is pretty big and there is no setting to make it smaller or dimmer- the new low power mode ( which I also do not want) dims the screen, removes my backgound picture ( which I want left up) and lo and behold, what is left glaringly bright on the screen - that digital clock in bright white big numbers! If I wanted a digital clock I would have purchased one. Asking this Echo to do the things I used to ask on my old little show5 is not intuitive at
summary: Review: 1/5. This was purchased to replace an Echo 5 that I loved, but had been bricked after the latest over -the- air update from Amazon. This one is not anything like my original. There is a giant digital clock that I cannot change… the old echo five had a setting for this- the background adjustment control has also disappeared! Now it is called clock and photo display- and there is NO setting for background!

product_title: Echo Show 8 (2nd Gen, 2021 release) - Glacier White - with 30-day FREE trial of Alexa Together (monthly auto-renewal)
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 3/5 Title: Honestly a bit disappointed. I was expecting more Text: I sort of purchased this on a whim to see if I could fit it into my life somehow. I found the idea of a smart display intriguing as I already have 2 Fire Cubes and absolutely love them. However a Fire cube this is not. It's difficult to express but this thing is cumbersome to use and it's slow. For example. I was eating dinner and watching a video I purchased on Prime video. I had to pause it to get up and do something so I said "echo pause" Wich it did. When I returned the screen had defaulted back to the time home screen. No big deal right? So I said "echo play" and instead of resuming my show, it resumed a song I had paused earlier that day. I asked it to stop and then asked for it to play my show. It began to play a different episode and not where I left off. Getting it back to where I left off required more awkward voice control and touching the screen. Very annoying when I'm trying to eat my meal. This device also has trouble understanding my queries. It's getting my words incorrect. One feature I was pleasently surprised by was the sound quality. It is very good. The low end is impressive. Almost as good as my Nest Audio. Maybe with some software updates this device Will be good. But for now I'm still trying to understand it's intended flow. Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: Good but can improve a lot Text: I just received this and was really excited for it. I bought this for my room because my roommate often stays up when he gets home to watch his shows, but I refuse to get a TV for my room. Using the other Alexa parts was just a plus for me, honestly.

Positives for me are the sound quality (amazing), voice recognition is better than on my old echo dot, and beautiful display.

Things that I hope Amazon listens to and improves…
- This is sort of difficult to navigate. I’m really good with technology but oh my god I can’t imagine my mother trying to work this thing. I’m okay with it, but it’s irritatingly annoying to use.
- Lots of streaming services are wonky,
summary: Reviewers were disappointed with the device. The sound quality was good, but not as good as my Nest Audio. The device is cumbersome to use and slow.

product_title: Echo Show 8 (2nd Gen, 2021 release) - Glacier White - with 30-day FREE trial of Alexa Together (monthly auto-renewal)
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: A MUST HAVE! Text: [[VIDEOID:b6537fe65ba9d68258a80970448c9f4a]] Gah! I love how big this screen is!!!! I've been suffering downstairs without an Alexa in my new house and decided decided to Gift myself a housewarming and so glad I did! My son is also loving watching cartoons during breakfast 😏. This speaker is SUPER oud. Definitely all you would need to throw a house party AND have the neighbors knocking 😂. It's chic and pretty! Review 2 Rating: 5/5 Title: A good system for what it does. Text: Amazon Echo Show 8 review:
Me, it's not what I thought it would be.
Also me, it's not a bad device.
Also, also me, It's not what you'd think.
You can remotely login and watch areas it's in, watch multiple cameras that are connected all over the place, look up content, listen to music (I'm listening to EYE OF THE TIGER) and it pretty much is a KARAOKE system, among other things.
In short, it's a smart speaker (yes, a "SMART SPEAKER"). You can't easily or at all, install third-party applications on it, requires the Alexa App, but if you want to control other Amazon/Alexa devices and such that are compatible, it's not a bad device.
I can control my TV (I will not explain that, too much of a pain in the ass), lights, and other items just from it. It's a great "hub", or a damn good device. For now, it's on sale, and for AP ("Amazon Prime") members, there's a discount.
For recipes, watching videos, among other things, it's a good device. Would I go against buying it? Depends what you're expecting from it, otherwise, it's not a bad investment -- and a good gift, especially for the elderly that want something simple.
I've been using mine for the following:
1. Tracking weather
2. Managing devices (near and far)
3. Monitoring my room when I am not home. (Alexa app required)
4. Listening to music (The sound is great for those that don't mind the best, or care for the best, etc., and better than low-end IMHO).
5. Watching movies (Halloween Ends, GOOD!) But I switched to my T V, because I ended up having to lay down.
6. It DOES NOT COME WITH A BATTERY (extra item sold seprately) and a STAND that is a separate purchase, but does well
summary: The Amazon Echo Show is a smart speaker with a large screen. It can be used to track weather, watch multiple cameras, and listen to music. It requires an Alexa app to be used.

product_title: Soundcore Anker Life P2 True Wireless Earbuds, Clear Sound, USB C, 40H Playtime, IPX7 Waterproof, Wireless Earphones for Work, Home Office
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: Life P2 is C2 for your Ears Text: While i have had Anker Soundcore LifeP2 for about month, I will only speak to how these earbuds have performed thus far. For the month I have had these, I will say this is the best quality earbuds I have had. If Anker charged me $170 for these buds, I would've shelled out the money regardless. Thats how good a product this is. Again Im not much for the idea of something fitting in your budget, because if its important you'll spend what you think its good for you and you want the buds to perform at its best and purest form for at least beyond the length of its warranty. Anker has always made a sound quality product and have set the price for its products that makes it affordable for most if not all.

What i love most about the Life P2 is its versatility. I have used these ear buds to make calls through my Iphone, which i never really did with previous earbuds Ive had because the mic quality on most is garbage, and the ability to hear whoever is on the other line is distant meaning that from an audio perspective the acoustics for voice speaking wasn't adequate and they'd only be good for music.

The Life P2 is good on all fronts. Crisp sound to hear, and clarity to speak.

Ive used the Life P2 for Zoom calls and people have said that my voice is more clear and better than the internal mics that are used the MacBook Airs.

The Life P2 is actually a really solid pair of earbuds for physical activity use & workouts. Since COVID-19 Ive done personal workouts with my Trainer using the P2's via Zoom outdoors and have been able to hear and speak with clarity. That says a lot for me because when you're outside ambient noises distort and distract but no issues there. Their noise cancellation isn't stellar but for me i think its more than adequate for myself and does the needful.

So i feel like Ive put the Life P2 to the test in accordance to my usage-lifestyle.

I will say that the only drawback is the distance. How far away can i move with the earbuds away from my cell before it cuts out.
summary: The Life P2 is good on all fronts. Crisp sound to hear, and clarity to speak. Their noise cancellation isn't stellar but for me i think its more than adequate for myself and does the needful.

combined_reviews:
NEGATIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 1/5 Title: magnet is weak, poor build qualty Text: Overall, the case looks nice and it reasonably protects an iPad Pro. There are two problem, though. First, the magnet is far too weak to lock the case open or closed. The cover will flop. Second, the build of the cover uses a method where a sturdy core is sandwiched between two soft layers. This creates rough edges, and tends to also result in splitting due to the constant bending the edges receive. While I am fully aware of how cheap this case is, I feel other cheap cases I have tried do not suffer from these problems.

Update: 6 months and corners are cracking. Certainly will not last another 6 months.

Final Update: After 7 months, the plastic edges around the corners of the case cracked badly in multiple places. But if I turn the ipad upside down, the front cover will drop because the magnet to trigger standby/ON is not designed to be strong enough to hold the weight of itself. If it is that strong, it will take more effort to flip cover open for normal use and this could be irritating. The cover can be easily flipped to the back and still maintains a flatness which for me is a plus. Some other covers material are so stiff that it is difficult to flip cover to the back or it leaves an unsightly bulge at the bend.

product_title: iXCC Element II Lightning Cable 6ft, iPhone Charger, for iPhone 7 6s 6 Plus, SE 5s 5c 5, iPad Air 2 Pro, iPad Mini 2 3 4, iPad 4th Gen [Apple MFi Certified](White)
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 3/5 Title: Consistently Fails After About 18 months Text: I have owned more than a dozen and a half of these cables, about half of them in the 3-foot length and half in the 6-foot length.

These seem like good quality cables when they comes out of the package, and I suspect they are faithful to the original Apple design. It may be that the original Apple design is not very robust.

What I have found with every one of these cables is that they consistently fail after about 18 months, for one of two reasons: (a) the cable jacket frays and opens up at the Lightning end of the cable, exposing the inner conductors and eventually shorting out, or (b) the gold plating on the little fingers of the Lightning connector degrades and eventually leads to intermittent connections. In both cases, the symptom becomes obvious when the phone is plugged into a charger and the little "bong" sounds repeatedly. In some worse cases, there is no charging at all.

IXCC states that it offers a two-year warranty for these cables. I have exercised this two or three times in the past, and IXCC promptly sent replacements. However, I have recently sought to invoke this warranty for two cables that I bought at the end of 2016., both of which have failed, and despite two attempts to contact IXCC through Amazon I have received no response.

Thus, I have reduced my previous higher rating of this cable product line to three stars, reflecting the fact that (a) these cables only have a limited life span, and (b) the warranty appears to be meaningless. Anyone buying these cables should simply plan on paying for replacements some time during the second year of ownership.

I am gentle with these cables, and do not extensively flex or kink them. I do plug and unplug them, of course, which causes wear on the gold plating, but I can't help that.

I don't know if any other brand of cable would hold up better than these. Again, it may be that Apple's design is inherently flawed. I simply feel that potential buyers should be aware of the limited life of these cables. Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: Lasts about a year Text: I bought two of these in March 2018. I left one in my car

combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 4/5 Title: It's a keeper for me, great set for the money! Text: [[VIDEOID:3048eeabf820c2c52afc67e1352e8042]] I was hesitant about buying this keyboard and mouse set due to some of the reviews, and the fact that I am very picky about my computer input devices. Honestly, I pulled the trigger based on looks, as I wanted a white and silver set to blend in with a room that was almost all white. There just aren't many keyboards/mice that are this color, so I figured I should give it a go. Well, so far, I am glad I did.

Measurements: The keyboard is approximately 14.5" wide and 5.5 inches tall. The thickness (from your table to the top of the keyboard) is roughly 3/4" at the top of the keyboard, to 1/4" at the bottom, so the keyboard is slightly tilted. The mouse is about 4.5" from top to bottom, and 2.5" from side to side. The height is 1/4" at the front tip of the mouse, approximately 3/4" in the middle, and a bit over 1/2" at the bottom.It is difficult to measure the height, as it is curved to fit the hand, but to me it is a flatter feel that the Microsoft mouse I was using before.I thought this would bug me, but it's actually way more comfortable.

Weight: I don't have a scale for actual weight, but the keyboard is light enough to carry with one hand easily, though not so lightweight that it moves or slides around on my slick wooden table. It does not feel cheap to me, and seems well made. The mouse is very lightweight, slides around my table easily without a mousepad, only has a barely detectable rattle when shaken.

Lag: There is no lag with the mouse at all. It is quick to move around the screen, zips along like a speeding bullet. The keyboard has no lag that I can tell.

Keyboard sounds: Many people have said this is "quiet" and some people have said it's noisy and clacky. To me, it's more of a thunk but not quite. It's not totally quiet, but it's not annoying me either. I'd say it's a matter of tolerance for anyone. I added a video so you could hear the keyboard. My phone is right next to it, and the room is perfectly quiet so you might get
summary: Review: It's a keeper for me, great set for the money! The keyboard is light enough to carry with one hand easily. The mouse is very lightweight, slides around my table easily without a mousepad, only has a barely detectable rattle when shaken.

In the end, I was getting the best sound from a large pair of tips that I got at Radio Shack when I lost a tip on an airplane.

In the end, what is causing me to return these is the battery life. I have used them during 10 workouts and the best I have achieved (starting from a full charge) is 2 hours and 23 minutes. I can live with having to charge them daily, but sometimes I am at the gym 3 to 4 hours and I can't have my earbuds going home before I do. I know volume can affect battery life, but in order to achieve the 2:23, I kept the volume a click or two lower than I would have liked.

On a side note, when I first purchased these, I received 3 e-mails from 7Desk/Phaiser support saying if I had any issue that would have me considering leaving a less-than-stellar review on Amazon, I should contact them directly. Well, I sent 2 e-mails back detailing the issues I was experiencing and have had no response whatsoever. So I have just printed out my Amazon return label. Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: They were OK. using a current limited power supply set to 5.0 Volts @ 1Amp, the internal battery can be charged in less
than 4 hours. (It just makes the math very easy to charge with a current limited supply)

5.0 Volts X 1.0Amp X 4 hours = 20 Watt-hours

At full volume, unit can play for about 7 hours (Not very loud, my work environment is very noisy)

So, the 20 Watt-hours are consumed in 7 hours (it plays at constant volume until the end or very close to constant)

20/7 = 2.8571 Watts/hour

So the battery is discharged at a rate of about 2.86 Watts/hour

But the 2.86 Watts is NOT pure sound!!!

It
summary: Reviews: 1/5, 2/5 and 3/5. Doesn't even turn on after a few weeks of use, sounds like tin can. Not very loud, my work environment is very noisy.

Review 1 Rating: 3/5 Title: Needs more volume increments Text: I’d like to compare the Vanzon Climber-Z speaker to the UE Boom 2 LE, which have almost identical form factors, but quite different (retail) prices. Both are somewhat heavy in the hand and crank out the decibels.

It is genuinely loud and I only had it up to the 5th or 6th volume setting. OTHERWISE I would recommend the UE Boom 2 LE, since it has much more precise volume control at the lower volumes, which is useful if you tend to prefer your music to stay in the background, or don’t want to bother other people but is still loud enough to be satisfying. In particular, with the Vanzon speaker, between volume setting 2 and 3 the loudness practically doubles, which I found bothersome. There need to be more increments of loudness in this area. Where there are 6 increments of volume at the quiet end with the UE Boom 2 LE, there are only 2 with the Vanzon, so you can’t “fine tune” the volume with the Vanzon in this loudness region, and for me this was a DEAL BREAKER.

Of note is the volume control, which is a dial that rotates at the top of the speaker, which is very convenient.

combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: READ ME IF THEY DON'T FIT PERFECTLY OR YOU WANT MORE CLARITY AND BASE!!!! Text: Review:
Best Bluetooth headset available because of fit, sound and ability to STAY in your ears and around your head.
Headset DOESN'T bother back of neck and essentially FLOATS around your head.
MUCH better than other loose cord headsets like Jaybirds that constantly fall out. This cord if just flexible enough and not too stiff.
Sound is GREAT for this price range and can be even BETTER if you follow this 1 suggestion

1. BUY "ENDURANCE SERIES ENHANCERS SIZE 5", THEY ARE ONLY $10 FOR 2 PAIRS FROM JBL!!! Size 5 is the most common size and fits right over the stock BackBeat bud. This Enhancer bud extends the "tube" that goes into your ear canal and allows for a more secure and solid connection. This connection increases EVERYTHING from BASE to TREBLE and just overall fit. There are a few different types of "Enhancers" so you can experiment with them. In order to get them to fit over the BackBeat bud you have to cut a little slit in the back for the "loop" to go through. Honestly some of the smaller Enhancers can all out replace the stock BackBeat bud instead of modifying it but both together fits best for me. To get the cut right just line the "tube" up on both buds than mark on the Enhancer where the "loop" comes out than make a SMALL single cut slit. Put the Enhancer on top of the bud than pull the loop through, this should help make the slit bigger without causing you to make to large of a hole. IMO its worth it for $5 a piece to experiment with this. I am including a few pictures to help explain what I have done here.

Conclusion: This additional purchase/modification has turned these 9.5/10 headphones into perfect 10's for me. I'm not sure how well it works on uncoating glass surfaces, but I recommend that it not be used on phones, pads, or optically coated surfaces or devices, such as lenses, binoculars, etc.. Review 2 Rating: 1/5 Title: I would pay someone NOT to use this product on my TV Text: I have a Samsung Q90R TV, it is the newest model with the anti-reflective coating layer. I am not sure if that's what caused my issue or if WHOOSH! is really just a bad cleaner for TVs in general. As soon as I applied WHOOSH! to my microfiber cloth and wiped the TV I was in instant regret. There were hazy streaks everywhere. I finally got my TV clean using 99% isopropyl alcohol but it took almost 40 minutes to clear the residue WHOOSH! left on my TV.

Please learn from my experience and avoid this product at all costs. Thank you Amazon for accepting my return of this junk. Review 3 Rating: 1/5 Title: Disappointed with the results and price Text: My vehicle manufacturer recommends computer screen cleaner to clean the display in my car. Not wanting to void the warranty, I bought Whoosh for that purpose. After using Whoosh on my car's display, home PC monitor, and smartphone, I can report that streaks were left on all three devices. I had to go back over all three with conventional glass cleaner to remove the streaks. Really wish I had done some research before buying snake
summary: Review 1: Works well Initially, then Makes Screen Harder to Clean. Review 2: It took almost 40 minutes to clear the residue WHOOSH! left on my TV. Review 3: Disappointed with the results and price.

combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: Works Great safe for electronics recommended by Apple. Text: Hello
I use your product daily, I am a long haul truck driver and I use it on my electronic Log Computer, my I Phone and , My Apple Watch . It was originally recommended to me by an Apple Store in Madison Wisconsin.
My only negative on your product is that I feel it is over priced.
I think $9.99 would be much more fair and reasonable.
$19.99 is excessive for what it is.
It works extremely well and I am happy with the product.
This is my third bottle purchased and other than the bottles leaking a bit when they fell over in my semi going down the road I don’t want to be nit picky I should have had the bottle secured.
To summarize : Great Product !! Works Great !! Over Priced !! Good Bottle !! Great Microfiber towel Included !! Would buy more if I could !!
Thank You God Bless !! Review 2 Rating: 4/5 Title: Excellent cleaner, won't use anything else. Text: This is the only screen cleaner I will use on my screens (TV, Monitors, laptops, tablets, phones). I must admit though that I feel it is over-priced - especially the smaller sizes. I had picked up a smaller size on closeout at a store, and discovered then it does the best job. This 16oz size was dropped down to $20 for a bit, so I went ahead and got it, and even for a household of screens (25+) this should last a year, so I got it.

Like all similar cleaners it says to spray on the cloth, not the screen, but, well, it tends to get sprayed on the screen as often as not, and there has been no build up or issues from this. I accidentally used it on my glasses once, and don't recommend that usage. However, for screens .. whichever type, it's perfect, including both glass and matte screen protectors.

My grandsons phone and tablets get unimaginably mucky, and this Whoosh! cleaner is the only one that easily removes all the oil, grime and "whatever" off of them in just a few swipes of the micro fibre cloth. I set aside one cloth just for his devices, cuz, "ewww".

I will continue to use Whoosh! so I guess even if

product_title: iMBAPrice® 25 Feet Professional Quality Nickel Plated 3.5 mm Male/Female Stereo Audio Extension Cable
combined_reviews:
NEGATIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 2/5 Title: Unreliable Text: I bought three of them because of the low price and good user reviews. One of them simply doesn't work (no sound), and the other two mostly work, but have reliability problems (static noises and disconnections with movement). The jack/plug connections are loose, and can disconnect too easily.

Others seem to have had better luck with this product, and you may too, but I can't recommend it. To me, it has been a waste of time. Crappy products lowers people's life quality. Buyers (saying this to myself also) should pay for quality to give manufacturers who care about quality a chance. Review 2 Rating: 1/5 Title: Get something that works instead of this! Text: This is ridiculous. This is the first time in my life that a simple audio cable did not function right out of the package. I plugged it into my TV, and could barely hear anything through my headphones. If I turned the volume up, I could hear the background music and sounds, but the voices were very distorted. (Plugging headsets directly into TV eliminated these problems.) The female plug is also off. It requires a very tight snap in of either of my headsets. I feel like it may even break the headset plugs. Don't waste your time. NEUTRAL REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 3/5 Title: Know what type of headphones you want to use this with, and make sure they will work! (Only TRS, 2 black lines, will work) Text: This product works great, but you need to be wary of what headphones you're using it with! I didn't realize this only works with TRS headphones (meaning there are only 2 black lines on the male end of your headphones, see image) instead of TRRS headphones (which have 3 black lines). If you have headphones that have a microphone capability, they will be TRRS headphones and will not work with this. To get them to work, you can buy a TRRS Headset To TRS Headphones Adapter (I got some here: https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00MV2PDCA/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o01_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1), but that is an additional cost I wasn't expecting. iMBAPrice should warn users about this before purchasing. Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: Three Stars Text: feels super thin and cheap, which it is. Review 3 Rating: 3/5 Title: cable Text: Product arrived with an exposed slice through the outer cover on the male end of the cable. Bare wire can be seen for about a half an inch. Cable works fine but I need to repair so the cable will last. Any ideas? Thank you for your help. POSITIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 4/5 Title: A good price but not a secure fit Text: It was as advertised,2 12 foot cables. Only problem is I don't think the female part is 3.5mm.Every time I plug in the second cable to extend from one end of the room where the desktop computer is to the other side of the room,it unplugs very easily. You have to be very careful.It is not a secure fit. Review 2 Rating: 5/5 Title: Good quality at a reasonable price Text: Just what I was looking for. Light weight, small diameter 3.5mm extension cable for my head phones. Good quality at a reasonable price. Review 3 Rating: 5/5 Title: Five Stars Text: Exactly what I needed.
summary: The cable is too thin and cheap. The jack/plug connections are loose, and can disconnect too easily. The female plug is also off. It requires a very tight snap in of either of my headsets.

product_title: Beams MB3000 High Performance 500 Lumen Wireless Battery Powered Motion Sensing LED Dual Head Security Spotlight, Brown, 1-Pack
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: Big light in a little package! Text: [[VIDEOID:48cdda1357d81c9ed3d88cd6f3dd0c83]] We have been looking for a non-electrical porch light, as we have no outlet, and when I had the chance to review these, I figured there was nothing to loose, since you get 2 for the price of 1!

The lights DO require FOUR D size batteries, and it is recommended to put in brand new ones.The back is easy to open, you can use a dime and unscrew all 4 points to open the battery compartment.Note- unscrew ALL the way, the screws will not fall out, but will be hinged,so to speak. Once you put the batteries in, make sure you select which lighting preference you want- the selector is under the compartment. A bit of a pain, but I get why it is there. Then you just rescrew the cover on and place the light in a dark from for about 10 minutes, to induce the sensor to start working.

Once we put the light outside it took 2 different tries of passes before it started working, but once it did, WOW. This light is BRIGHTER than any of the sensor lights we have outside the house already! It easily picks up when someone opens the porch door and comes on, providing plenty of illumination to see to the front door, and unlock it. In fact you get about 15 seconds more light once you get in, before it automatically goes off. And it is not super sensitive, ie moths do not set it off! However repeated movement will keep it lit!

Because it is SO bright we ended up ONLY needing one on the porch, and the other we were able to put on the carport. Now between the 2 there is more than enough light to safely get in the house, and to see anyone who might be lurking outside!

We highly recommend these little lights- they are perfect for placing where YOU need light!

I was super surprised with JUST how well these battery powered lights work!

UPDATE:

The batteries lasted about 10 months, thanks to a squirrel and bird who both got on the porch and made nests. UGH. their motion constantly set off the lights, hence the batteries didnt last the year as expected..These are the BEST motion lights we've EVER had! Review
summary: The lights require FOUR D size batteries, and it is recommended to put in brand new ones. The batteries lasted about 10 months, thanks to a squirrel and bird who both got on the porch and made nests. These are the BEST motion lights we've EVER had!
